checks.name: trigger_validity
checks.detail: 3 triggers: webhook, weekly Monday 9AM schedule, manual

dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity >50 words. Instructions >200 words with 4 core responsibilities. Tool guidance with LaunchDarkly/Slack/Linear/Notion API specs. Three examples (creation, modification, weekly scan, collision). Error handling with LD/Slack/Notion/Linear errors. Two custom sections for naming conventions and memory schema.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: http_request mapped to LaunchDarkly REST v2, Slack Block Kit, Linear GraphQL, Notion pages/databases API. file_read/write for local state. No phantom tools.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: Three triggers: webhook for real-time flag events, weekly Monday 9AM for stale scan, manual for full registry sync. All match corresponding prompt responsibilities.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: LaunchDarkly connector is not in builtin but is swappable to PostHog via the feature_flags role in connectorRoles.ts. Slack, Linear, Notion all have builtin connectors.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: Agent memory schema comprehensively documented in custom section: notion_flag_db_id, linear_team_id, linear_label_id, slack_audit_channel, ld_project_keys, stale_threshold_days, last_weekly_scan.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: LaunchDarkly 401/429/5xx, Slack message queuing fallback, Notion abort with alert, Linear duplicate prevention, webhook deduplication via local state, missing config detection with user_message.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: All 4 core responsibilities covered: flag change audit, naming collision prevention, stale flag cleanup, Notion registry maintenance with reconciliation.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Replaces 4 n8n workflows. Governance enforcement (naming, collision detection), cleanup accountability (Linear tickets), audit trail (Notion registry), real-time visibility (Slack).

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: LaunchDarkly requires connector swap to PostHog. Notion, Linear, Slack APIs are standard. Webhook, schedule, and manual triggers are all actionable.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: Naming collision detection with near-match analysis (shared first 3 tokens), naming convention enforcement, registry reconciliation between LaunchDarkly and Notion.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: Adoption questions cover project keys, DB ID, team ID, channel, threshold, and naming enforcement toggle. All genuinely needed for governance operation.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Notion DB ID and Linear team ID have empty defaults requiring mandatory user input. LD project keys (default) and stale threshold (90) have sensible defaults.

issues: No adoption_questions existed before fix - LD project keys, Notion DB ID, Linear team ID, Slack channel, threshold all needed user-specific configuration
LaunchDarkly connector not in builtin - swappable to PostHog via feature_flags role in connectorRoles.ts
